ingredients.name: 7-Eleven Shinjuku Yasukuni Ave.
ingredients.localName: セブン-イレブン 新宿駅靖国通り店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア
ingredients.address: 小松ビル 1F, 1 Chome-17-2 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Taki searches desperately for Mitsuha in Shinjuku, and the area around this convenience store captures his frantic late-night wandering through a city that holds memories he cannot quite place.

ingredients.name: Kita-sando Sta.
ingredients.entityContext: The Kita-sando station area appears in background shots as Taki travels through Tokyo during his search for Mitsuha, grounding the film's Tokyo sequences in recognisable commuter infrastructure.

ingredients.name: Kinokuni-zaka Slope
ingredients.entityContext: This gently curving slope in Akasaka appears during Mitsuha-as-Taki's walks through Tokyo, one of several Akasaka streets Shinkai used to contrast the city's quiet backstreets with the bustle of Shinjuku.

ingredients.name: Jingu Softball Stadium Fountain
ingredients.entityContext: The fountain plaza near Meiji Jingu Gaien appears in the film as a backdrop during Taki's daily life in Tokyo, part of the familiar urban landscape that Mitsuha experiences when she swaps into his body.

ingredients.name: History of old Akasaka area
ingredients.entityContext: This historical marker near Kioicho sits within the Akasaka district that Shinkai used extensively for Tokyo street-level scenes, appearing in background shots as characters navigate the city.

ingredients.name: Hidashi Information Centre
ingredients.entityContext: The Hida City Tourist Information Centre in Furukawa is the real-world counterpart to the sightseeing office Mitsuha visits, and pilgrims stop here to pick up Your Name. location maps distributed to fans.

ingredients.name: Hida-Furukawa Station
ingredients.entityContext: Hida-Furukawa Station is the arrival point for fans making the pilgrimage to Itomori's real-world model town; its charming single-platform wooden structure matches the rural station seen when Taki and his friends arrive in search of Mitsuha.

ingredients.name: Hida City Library
ingredients.entityContext: The Hida City Library in Furukawa served as a visual reference for Itomori's public buildings; pilgrims recognise its quiet exterior in the film's depiction of the small-town civic spaces Mitsuha moves through.

ingredients.name: HANAGATAYA Tokyo Yaesu South Exit
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒100-0005 Tokyo, Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−9−1 JR Tokyo Station, １階 八重洲南口改札内
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Station's souvenir shops inside the Yaesu exits are depicted when Taki's friend Okudera-senpai works there, and the film's early sequences establish this as the bustling heart of Taki's Tokyo world.

ingredients.name: HANAGATAYA Tokyo Station South (Bento)
ingredients.address: 南通路 JR Tokyo Station, １Ｆ, 1 Chome-9-1 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bento shop inside Tokyo Station's south concourse is part of the station complex where Okudera-senpai works, a backdrop for the film's glimpses of Taki's admiration for her.

ingredients.name: Entsuji-zaka
ingredients.entityContext: Entsuji-zaka is one of the Akasaka-area slopes that Shinkai included in the film's Tokyo street scenes, part of the hilly residential fabric that Taki and Mitsuha-as-Taki navigate.

ingredients.name: Ekibenya Matsuri Gransta Tokyo
ingredients.localName: 駅弁屋 祭 グランスタ東京
ingredients.address: JR Tokyo Station, 構内, 1 Chome-9-1 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The famous ekiben shop inside Tokyo Station's Gransta concourse mirrors the type of station-interior location depicted when Taki visits Tokyo Station looking for clues about Mitsuha.

ingredients.name: Cafe La Boheme Shinjuku Gyoen
ingredients.localName: カフェ ラ・ボエム 新宿御苑
ingredients.address: コスモ新宿御苑ビル 1F・2F, 1 Chome-1-7 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Cafe La Boheme near Shinjuku Gyoen appears to be linked to the dating scene where Taki (inhabited by Mitsuha) takes Okudera-senpai out, with the Shinjuku Gyoen neighbourhood providing the backdrop for their awkward outing.

ingredients.name: polyphony
ingredients.type: オーガニック レストラン
categories: オーガニック レストラン
ingredients.address: ベルビー四谷 1階, 1 Chome-21-2 Wakaba, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0011, Japan

ingredients.name: Meiji Jingu Gaien
ingredients.entityContext: The tree-lined ginkgo avenue of Meiji Jingu Gaien provides a backdrop for Tokyo autumn scenes in the film, part of the seasonal Tokyo imagery Shinkai layers through Taki's everyday life.

ingredients.name: CELINE Omotesando
ingredients.address: One Omotesando, 1F, 3 Chome-5-29 Kita-Aoyama, Minato City, Tokyo 107-0061, Japan

ingredients.name: Benkei Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-2 Motoakasaka, Minato City, Tokyo 107-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Benkei Bridge over the moat near Akasaka appears in the film's Akasaka-district walking sequences, its reflection in the water lending the city a dreamy quality consistent with Shinkai's visual style.

ingredients.name: Ajidokoro Furukawa
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional set-meal restaurant in Furukawa's historic Ichinomachi streetscape is part of the old-town atmosphere that inspired Itomori's townscape, and fans visit it after the Hida-Furukawa pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Nagoya Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-4 Meieki, Nakamura Ward, Nagoya, Aichi, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nagoya Station serves as a transit hub for fans travelling between Tokyo and Hida-Furukawa, and it appears briefly in the film as part of Taki's train journey toward Mitsuha's town.

ingredients.name: Near the Cafe Veloce Nishi-Shinjuku store
ingredients.localName: カフェ・ベローチェ西新宿店付近
ingredients.address: 加賀谷ビル 1F, 7 Chome-10-7 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The streets of Nishi-Shinjuku near this corner appear in the film during Taki's search through Shinjuku as he tries to find any trace of Mitsuha, the looming skyscrapers amplifying his sense of dislocation.

ingredients.name: Now On Cheese LUMINE Shinjuku
ingredients.localName: ナウ オン チーズ ルミネ新宿店
ingredients.type: デザート ショップ
categories: デザート ショップ
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒160-0022 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−2 ルミネ新宿店, ルミネ2 1階

ingredients.name: Ochiai busstop
ingredients.entityContext: The Ochiai bus stop in the Hida highlands appears in the film when Taki's group alights here to begin their trek toward the crater lake that corresponds to Itomori, a key moment in their real-world search.

ingredients.name: Robert Indiana Sculpture: "LOVE"
ingredients.address: 6 Chome-5-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The LOVE sculpture in Nishi-Shinjuku's plaza appears in the film's Shinjuku street scenes, one of the recognisable public artworks in the urban environment Taki inhabits.

ingredients.name: SEIBU Shinjuku PePe & Brick St.
ingredients.localName: 西武新宿ペペ & Brick St.
ingredients.type: ショッピング モール
categories: ショッピング モール
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-30-1 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The area outside Seibu Shinjuku Station, including the Brick St. pedestrian zone, is depicted during Taki's evening explorations of Kabukicho and the entertainment district as he searches for Mitsuha.

ingredients.name: Saika Park Shinanomachi Station Bicycle Parking Area A
ingredients.localName: サイカパーク信濃町駅駐輪場Aエリア
ingredients.entityContext: The Shinanomachi area near this bicycle park appears in scenes around Meiji Jingu Gaien, part of the route Taki walks during his daily Tokyo life and a quiet residential corner Shinkai used for background shots.

ingredients.name: Sendagaya Station
ingredients.entityContext: Sendagaya Station and its surroundings near Meiji Jingu Gaien feature in the film's Tokyo street sequences, with the station's overhead rail structure visible in background cuts during Taki's commute.

ingredients.name: Shibuya Station West Exit Pedestrian Bridge Deck
ingredients.localName: 渋谷駅西口歩道橋デッキ

ingredients.name: Shinanomachi Footbridge
ingredients.address: Shinanomachi Footbridge, 14-9 Kasumigaokamachi, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shinanomachi Footbridge is one of the pedestrian overpasses near Meiji Jingu Gaien that appears in the film, visible in shots where Taki looks out over the city's rail lines and rooftops.

ingredients.name: Shinjuku Building
ingredients.type: ビジネス センター
categories: ビジネス センター
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-8-1 Nishishinjuku, 新宿区 Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shinjuku Building on Nishishinjuku's main boulevard is part of the skyscraper cluster that frames the film's Tokyo skyline shots, a recognisable element of Taki's Shinjuku backdrop.

ingredients.name: Site of the Kuichigai-Mitsuke
ingredients.entityContext: This historic Edo-period guardpost site near Kioicho is embedded within the Akasaka walking route Shinkai used for Tokyo street scenes, adding a layer of historical depth to the film's urban imagery.

ingredients.name: Starbucks Coffee - Shibuya Tsutaya 2F
ingredients.localName: スターバックス コーヒー SHIBUYA TSUTAYA 2F店

ingredients.name: Suga Shrine Otokodan
ingredients.entityContext: The stone staircase at Suga Shrine is the single most iconic location in the film: it is the spot where Taki and Mitsuha finally see each other on the steps and Mitsuha asks him to write his name, the emotional climax of their story.

ingredients.name: The National Art Center Tokyo
ingredients.address: 7 Chome-22-2 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-8558, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The National Art Center's distinctive undulating glass facade in Roppongi appears in background establishing shots of Tokyo, part of the contemporary city architecture Shinkai incorporates into Taki's world.

ingredients.name: Tofukuin-zaka
ingredients.address: 15 Chome-778 Honmachi, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0981, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tofukuin-zaka, a short slope in the Yotsuya-Suga area just steps from Suga Shrine, is included in the film's depiction of the hilly neighbourhood Taki inhabits, and fans walk it as part of the Yotsuya pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Tokyo City View
ingredients.localName: 六本木ヒルズ展望台 東京シティビュー
ingredients.address: Roppongi Hills Mori Tower, 52階, 6 Chome-10-1 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The observation deck atop Roppongi Hills Mori Tower provides a panoramic view of Tokyo that echoes the sweeping aerial shots Shinkai uses to convey the vast, lonely city that separates Taki and Mitsuha.

ingredients.name: Tower Records Shibuya
ingredients.localName: タワーレコード 渋谷店

ingredients.name: VIEW ALTTE Next to the ticket machine at the south exit of JR Shinjuku Station
ingredients.localName: VIEW ALTTE JR新宿駅南口券売機横
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒160-0022 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−1
ingredients.entityContext: The south exit ticket machine area of JR Shinjuku Station is the site of a pivotal scene where Taki waits for Mitsuha and the two nearly miss each other on the stairs, capturing the film's bittersweet near-encounter.

ingredients.name: Yotsuya Station
ingredients.entityContext: Yotsuya Station features prominently in the film's climactic sequence: Mitsuha spots a stranger on a passing train who looks like Taki, and later the two meet on the station staircase in the final scene, making this one of the film's most emotionally resonant real locations.

ingredients.name: Yoyogi Station
ingredients.entityContext: Yoyogi Station and its surroundings appear in background shots of Taki's Tokyo commute, part of the Chuo Line corridor that Shinkai used to build the texture of everyday student life in the city.

ingredients.name: i-Shop Ishido
ingredients.localName: アイショップ 石堂店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア
ingredients.address: 2172-1 Noma, Nakatane, Kumage District, Kagoshima 891-3604, Japan
